description: former U.S. Army training facility
description_wiki: Camp Hale was a U.S. Army training facility in the western United States, constructed in 1942 for what became the 10th Mountain Division.

description_wiki: Fort Benning (formerly Fort Moore from 2023–25) is a United States Army post in the Columbus, Georgia, area.

seat of Travis County, and capital of the State of Texas
description_wiki: Austin (  AW-stin) is the capital city of the U.S. state of Texas.

description: city and comune in Tuscany, Central Italy
description_wiki: Lucca ( LOO-kə, Italian: [ˈlukka] ) is a city and comune in Tuscany, Central Italy, on the Serchio River, in a fertile plain.

description: German defensive line in Italy during World War II
description_wiki: The Gothic Line (German: Gotenstellung; Italian: Linea Gotica) was a German and Italian defensive line of the Italian Campaign of World War II. It formed Field Marshal Albert Kesselring's last major line of defence along the summits of the northern part of the Apennine Mountains during the fighting retreat of the Axis forces in Italy against the Allied Armies in Italy, commanded by General Sir Harold Alexander.

description: plain in Northern Italy
description_wiki: The Po Valley, Po Plain, Plain of the Po, or Padan Plain (Italian: Pianura Padana, pronounced [pjaˈnuːra paˈdaːna], or Val Padana) is a major geographical feature of northern Italy.

description: longest river in Italy
description_wiki: The Po ( POH, Italian: [ˈpɔ]) is the longest river in Italy.

surface_forms: the west side of Lake Garda
the other side of Lake Garda
description: lake in Italy
description_wiki: Lake Garda (Italian: Lago di Garda, Italian: [ˈlaːɡo di ˈɡarda], or (Lago) Benaco, Italian: [beˈnaːko]; Eastern Lombard: Lach de Garda; Venetian: Ƚago de Garda) is the largest lake in Italy.

description: dictator of Italy from 1922 to 1945
description_wiki: Benito Amilcare Andrea Mussolini (29 July 1883 – 28 April 1945) was an Italian politician, journalist, and dictator who led Italy as Il Duce from 1922 until his overthrow in 1943.

description: Atlantic Ocean archipelago and autonomous region of Portugal
description_wiki: The Azores, officially the Autonomous Region of the Azores, is an autonomous region of Portugal in the Atlantic Ocean about 1,400 km (870 miles) west of mainland Portugal.

description: capital city of Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima (広島市, Hiroshima-shi; English: , also UK: , US: , Japanese: [çiɾoɕima] ) is the capital of Hiroshima Prefecture in Japan.

description: core city in Kyushu, Japan
description_wiki: Nagasaki (長崎, Nagasaki; Japanese pronunciation: [na.ɡaꜜ.sa.kʲi, na.ŋa-], lit. 'Long Cape'), officially Nagasaki City (長崎市, Nagasaki-shi; [na.ɡa.sa.kʲi̥ꜜ.ɕi, na.ɡa.saꜜ.kʲi̥.ɕi, na.ŋa-]), is the capital and the largest city of Nagasaki Prefecture on the island of Kyushu in Japan.

description: American automobile division of GM
description_wiki: Chevrolet is an American automobile division of the manufacturer General Motors (GM).

aliases: Act to provide Federal Government aid for the readjustment in civilian life of returning World War II veterans
description: United States law that provided a range of benefits for returning World War II veterans
description_wiki: A G.I. Bill is a colloquial name given to several pieces of legislation that have provided a range of benefits for American military veterans, particularly education benefits.

description: former fort in Salt Lake City, Utah, United States
description_wiki: Fort Douglas (initially called Camp Douglas) was established in October 1862, during the American Civil War, as a small military garrison about three miles east of Salt Lake City, Utah.

description: city in Washoe County, Nevada, United States
description_wiki: Sparks is a city in Washoe County, Nevada, United States.
